meaning: adj.上游的,向水源的adv.在上游,向上游,从上游
examples: Heavy goods could be brought upriver in barges.
Steamboats were not only faster but also transported upriver freight for about one-tenth of what it had previously cost on hand-propelled keelboats.
News has been coming upriver, none of it good.
Further upriver, Marie leads the way in a thrilling scramble though a maze of overground caverns.
That was allowing saltwater to begin working its way upriver, which could threaten some water supplies.
